choices: Holdout rule implemented exactly as: exclude phrases where int(hashlib.sha1(clinical_prompt.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest(), 16) % 10 == 0 (full SHA1 hex digest as integer, mod 10); this convention was validated against the row file's own tierb_split labels — it reproduces all 224 'holdout' rows with 0 false positives and 0 misses (an alternate first-8-hex-chars convention does not match).

The medgemma-4b-it rows are all from Tier A batch pairs_20260707T171223Z and carry no tierb_split tag (the prereg amendment scopes the holdout to Tier B pairs); per instruction I applied the hash rule to them anyway, excluding 11 of 119 phrases, leaving 108.

Population = all 119 rows with model=='medgemma-4b-it' in /home/user/patientwords-engine/urgency_shift.json; clinical_prompt is present directly on each row, so no join to simulated_scenarios.json or batch files was needed for prompt text.
No duplicates existed by clinical_prompt or (batch,index), so the mean-of-duplicates (probabilities) and majority-vote (flip direction) collapse rules were implemented but were no-ops.

Penalty per row = the 'language_penalty' field; verified in traces that it equals probabilities.patient minus probabilities.clinical for the clinical target token (prob of clinical next word under patient wording minus under clinical wording, matching the written definition). Mean over deduped kept phrases x100, 1 decimal: -3.3 pp (raw -3.3222).

Downgrades/upgrades taken from the per-row 'flip_class' classification field already present (values: downgrade/upgrade/lateral/uninformative/None), phrase-deduped: 7 downgrades, 2 upgrades among the 108 kept phrases.

The row file has no fields literally named prob_clinical/prob_patient; I verified p_top_clinical/p_top_patient, which equal the top-1 probability of the clinical/patient predictive_spread in the original trace, rounded to 3 decimals (tolerance 5e-4); language_penalty was also cross-checked per sampled row.
Sampling: random.seed(42); random.sample(sorted(kept_phrases), 15) — phrases sorted alphabetically before sampling for determinism.

Trace join: /home/user/patientwords-engine/trace_out/pairs_20260707T171223Z__medgemma-4b-it/batch_summary.part_01.json (119 results), joined on results[i]['index'] == row['index'] with prompts.clinical asserted equal to row clinical_prompt on every sampled row.
